title: Corporate governance
explanation:
Corporate governance

  • shareholder, accountability, compliance, audit, fiduciary
  • board, transparency, misconduct, stakeholder, oversight
  • verbs: delegate, disclose, regulate, scrutinise

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The scandal raised questions about executive ____.  | *accountability | ability | availability
____ require accurate financial reporting. | *Shareholders | Shareholds | Sharing
The firm failed ____ with safety regulations. | *compliance | complaint | compliment
Independent ____ reviewed the company's accounts. | *auditors | authors | actors

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: Directors have a ____ duty to act in the firm's interest. | fiduciary
The report calls for greater ____ in public spending. | transparency
Regulators will ____ the merger closely. | scrutinise
Managers must ____ conflicts of interest. | disclose
The board decided to ____ more authority to regional heads. | delegate

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Strong ____ helps prevent fraud. | *oversight | overview | overtime
Employees are important ____ in any reform. | *stakeholders | stockholders only | strangers
Alleged financial ____ is under investigation. | *misconduct | misconnect | misplace
Governments ____ industries to protect consumers. | *regulate | recycle | resign
